name: Aunt Eloise's Borscht
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon dried basil, rubbed to powder
1/2 teaspoon dried oregano, rubbed to powder
3 Sour leaves or juice of 1/2 lemon
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare sour leaves (or use sorrel leaves) as noted.
2. Place the cubed potatoes in a pot and add 12 cups water. Bring to a boil.
3. Melt margarine in a frying pan and sauté onion until brown.
4. Add tomatoes.
5. Peel beets and julienne them. Leave the root and 2-3 inches of stem on the beets to prevent color bleeding.
6. Add potatoes and bring back to a boil.
7. Add tomatoes and onions to the mixture, then simmer.
8. Add cabbage.
9. Stir in parsley, basil, oregano, and sour leaves or lemon juice.
10. Optional: Simmer pork bones in water, remove bones and scum, then add potatoes and beets. Include garlic with tomatoes, or add lentils/navy beans for variety.
11. Serve hot or cold with sour cream.
Aunty Eloise notes that different regions may use lighter borscht in summer and richer versions in winter. Some families use sour salt instead of sour leaves.
